title: Broken import: generateSlug imported from wrong module
description: `import { generateSlug } from '@/lib/markdown'` — but `generateSlug` is exported from `@/lib/slug`, not `@/lib/markdown`. At runtime, POST /api/wiki will throw `TypeError: generateSlug is not a function`, breaking all wiki page creation through this (bulk, non-project-scoped) route.
suggested_fix: Change the import to `import { generateSlug, uniqueSlug } from '@/lib/slug'` and apply the same uniquification pattern used in `pages/api/projects/[projectId]/wiki/index.ts` (find existing slugs in project, then call `uniqueSlug` to append `-2`, `-3`, etc.).

title: Broken import: generateSlug imported from wrong module
description: Same defect as WIKI-1: `generateSlug` is imported from `@/lib/markdown` but lives in `@/lib/slug`. Any PATCH on /api/wiki/[id] that changes the title will throw at runtime, blocking all wiki renames via the bulk route.
suggested_fix: Change to `import { generateSlug, uniqueSlug } from '@/lib/slug'`. When title changes, also call `uniqueSlug(baseSlug, existingSlugs)` so renaming "Foo" when "Foo-2" exists auto-suffixes to "Foo-3" instead of failing with 409.

title: Cross-project privacy leak: slug lookup is not project-scoped
description: `findFirst({ where: { slug } })` filters ONLY by slug, not by projectId. The `assertWikiPageBySlugProjectMembership` helper gates access to the first page that matches globally — so a user who is a member of Project A can read the content of a wiki page in Project B if B's page has the same slug. This is a privacy bug because the helper is 'by-slug' but the schema requires slug to be project-scoped (`@@unique([projectId, slug])`).
suggested_fix: Either (a) require `projectId` as a query param and filter by `{ slug, projectId }`, or (b) delete this bulk endpoint and route callers to the project-scoped `/api/projects/[projectId]/wiki/[slug]`. Option (a) is the minimal fix.

title: Wiki macro fetches non-existent endpoint
description: Client code calls `fetch('/api/projects/${projectId}/wiki/by-slug?slug=...')` to resolve `[[include:PageName]]` macros. The project-scoped route does NOT exist — only `/api/wiki/by-slug` does (see WIKI-3). Every wiki page that uses the include macro will 404 in production.
suggested_fix: Either create `/api/projects/[projectId]/wiki/by-slug.ts` (project-scoped mirror of the bulk one) or update the client to call the bulk endpoint with projectId as a query param once that endpoint is project-scoped per WIKI-3.

title: renderMarkdownSync misnamed: does not parse Markdown
description:
`renderMarkdownSync` calls DOMPurify.sanitize directly on the input string with no unified/remark-parse pipeline. It accepts Markdown but returns sanitized RAW TEXT — `# Hello` is NOT converted to `

Hello

`. Any caller that uses this expecting rendered output gets plain text instead, breaking live preview / diff display.
suggested_fix: Either (a) use `unified().use(remarkParse).use(remarkGfm).use(remarkRehype).use(rehypeStringify).processSync(content)` (verify remark-rehype supports sync) and pipe through DOMPurify, or (b) rename to `sanitizeHtmlSync` and update the docstring. Do NOT keep the misleading name.

title: Slug uniqueness race condition under concurrent creates
description: Slug is computed via `generateSlug` + `uniqueSlug` over a snapshot of existing slugs, then `wikiPage.create` runs without a uniqueness retry. Two simultaneous POSTs with the same title both compute the same free slug, both pass the pre-check, and one fails with a 500 Prisma P2002 error instead of getting a clean -2 suffix. The uniqueSlug helper exists but is not retried after the DB write.
suggested_fix: Wrap the create in a loop: on Prisma P2002 (unique violation) re-run `uniqueSlug` against the just-fetched slugs and retry. Alternatively add `slug` to a short unique-violation retry helper. Use `prisma.$transaction` with `serializable` isolation or rely on retry-up-to-N-times.

title: parentId change has no cycle check — can create infinite hierarchy loop
description: PATCH allows `parentId` to be set to any wiki page in the project without verifying the new parent is not a descendant of the current page. A page can be set as its own ancestor (Page A → parent B, B → parent A, etc.), producing cycles that break the wiki tree / TOC and crash recursive rendering. The `pages/api/wiki/[id].ts` (bulk) variant has the same gap.
suggested_fix: Before applying `parentId`, walk up `parent.parentId` chain from the proposed new parent and reject (400) if `id` is found in the chain. Also reject if `parentId === id`.

title: Create-time parentId check is best-effort, no DB-level FK enforcement on WikiPage.parent
description: POST verifies `parent.projectId === projectId` at the application level (line 91-101). If the parent is concurrently deleted between this check and the wikiPage.create, the FK constraint will throw (since schema has no `onDelete` directive). The route returns 500 instead of a clean 400. Same race exists for PATCH parentId changes (WIKI-7).
suggested_fix: Wrap the create in a transaction that re-fetches the parent after acquiring it; or add explicit `onDelete: SetNull` to the WikiPage.parent relation so the DB handles it predictably.

title: Version row created on every PATCH, even title-only / parentId-only changes
description: `newVersion = currentPage.version + 1` runs unconditionally and `tx.wikiPageVersion.create({...content: body.content ?? currentPage.content})` runs on every PATCH. A user who only renames a page or moves it under a different parent creates a new version row with the OLD content — polluting the version history with non-content edits, and the version number no longer corresponds to a content change.
suggested_fix: Only increment `version` and create a `WikiPageVersion` when `body.content !== undefined && body.content !== currentPage.content`. Title/parentId changes should NOT bump the version. Match the project-scoped PATCH behavior at `pages/api/projects/[projectId]/wiki/[slug].ts:90`.

title: Version row authorId set to original author, not editor
description: `tx.wikiPageVersion.create({ authorId: currentPage.authorId, ... })` records the original page creator as the version author regardless of who actually made the edit. This corrupts the audit trail (Phase 4 spec §2.7 requires each version's actual editor). Same defect in `pages/api/wiki/[id]/restore.ts:59` and `pages/api/projects/[projectId]/wiki/[slug]/restore.ts:99`.
suggested_fix: Use `session.user.id` (the current editor) instead of `currentPage.authorId`. For restores, the editor IS the restorer.

title: Restore uses page.version + 1 instead of max(versions.version) + 1
description: `newVersion = page.version + 1` after a restore can collide with already-existing versions if intermediate edits had gaps, or worse: a restore creates version N+1 even if a version row for N+1 already exists (from a previous edit captured before the restore). The unique key on (wikiPageId, version) is not enforced — duplicate version rows corrupt history. Use `max(version) + 1` from the actual versions table.
suggested_fix: Compute `nextVersion = await tx.wikiPageVersion.aggregate({ where: { wikiPageId: id }, _max: { version: true } }) + 1`, then create the version row inside the same transaction.

title: Dead code: empty conditional with comment only
description: Lines 76-79: `if (target.version === page.version && target.content === '') { /* edge case comment */ }` — this block has no body, no `return`, no `throw`. It's leftover from a refactor. Dead code confuses readers and signals incomplete work.
suggested_fix: Delete the block. If the intended no-op early-return behavior is needed, write `if (target.version === page.version) return res.status(200).json({ success: true, data: page })`.

title: DELETE wiki page with children fails with generic 400 but no DB-level safety net
description: `if (page._count.children > 0)` blocks deletion — but the only enforcement is application-level. If children exist across multiple pages and are concurrently inserted, the count read is racy. Also: orphaned children will be left with a dangling `parentId` after deletion of parent if the check is bypassed. No FK `onDelete` rule is set on `WikiPage.parent` (schema `prisma/schema.prisma:525` — relation has no `onDelete` directive).
suggested_fix: Add `onDelete: SetNull` (or `Restrict`) on `parent WikiPage? @relation(..., onDelete: SetNull)` so the DB enforces integrity. Also wrap DELETE in a transaction that re-checks children after acquiring a row lock, or use a recursive CTE to delete the subtree.

title: DELETE is hard delete, cascades to versions silently
description: `prisma.wikiPage.delete({ where: { id: page.id } })` deletes the page and (per schema `onDelete: Cascade` on versions) all `WikiPageVersion` rows are removed. No soft-delete, no audit, no restore. The bulk `pages/api/wiki/[id].ts` DELETE behaves the same. Phase 4 spec §2.4 implies audit-trail preservation.
suggested_fix: Add a `deletedAt DateTime?` column to WikiPage (migration), change DELETE to set `deletedAt = now()` + emit activity, exclude from list queries, and keep versions intact.

title: POST /api/documents creates document with arbitrary folderId, no project-scope check
description: Body contains `folderId`, and the handler does NOT verify that the folder belongs to `body.projectId` before creating the document. A user can pass `projectId = A` and `folderId = <folder-in-project-B>` and the document will be linked to a foreign folder. Cross-project data association bug.
suggested_fix: After auth check, if `body.folderId` is set, fetch the folder and reject with 400 if `folder.projectId !== body.projectId`. Same fix as the project-scoped route `pages/api/projects/[projectId]/documents/index.ts:78-85` already has.

title: PATCH folderId has no project-scope check
description: Updating `folderId` on a document does not verify the folder belongs to the same project. A document in Project A can be moved into a folder in Project B via direct ID manipulation.
suggested_fix: After fetching the document, if `body.folderId` is set and not null, verify `folder.projectId === document.projectId`. Reject 400 if mismatched.

title: PATCH folder parentId allows cycle / cross-project move
description: Folder PATCH lets users set `parentId` to any folder id with no check that the new parent is in the same project AND not a descendant of this folder. Result: folder cycles (Folder A → parent B, B → parent A) cause infinite recursion in `FolderTree`. The `id !== parentId` self-check is there but the descendant-cycle check is missing.
suggested_fix: Walk the ancestor chain of the proposed `parentId` and reject if `id` is found (cycle). Also verify `parent.projectId === folder.projectId` (cross-project move prevention).

title: POST folder with parentId has no project-scope check
description: Body contains `projectId` + `parentId`; the handler does not verify the parent folder belongs to `body.projectId`. Folders can be created across projects via this loophole.
suggested_fix: After assertProjectMembership, if `body.parentId` is set, fetch the parent and reject 400 if `parent.projectId !== body.projectId`.

title: Preview tab is non-functional — shows raw Markdown source
description:
`{showPreview ? 
{content}
:
see exactly what they typed in monospace, defeating the purpose. There is no call to `renderMarkdown`.
suggested_fix: Replace with `<WikiMarkdown content={content} />` from `@/components/wiki/WikiMarkdown`, debounced while typing. Remove the misleading preview UI.

title: Slug collision on POST throws 409 instead of auto-uniquifying
description: When `existing` page with the same generated slug is found, the handler throws `WIKI_PAGE_EXISTS` 409. The project-scoped variant (`pages/api/projects/[projectId]/wiki/index.ts:104-113`) correctly applies `uniqueSlug` to suffix `-2`, `-3`, etc. Inconsistent UX — same title works in one route and fails in the other.
suggested_fix: Use `uniqueSlug(baseSlug, existing.map(p => p.slug))` from `@/lib/slug`. Fixes both the UX and the WIKI-1 broken-import.

title: Slug generator strips non-ASCII to empty — falls back to 'untitled'
description: `replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, '-')` strips CJK / Cyrillic / Arabic characters as 'non a-z0-9'. Two unrelated pages with titles "计划
计画

description.title: Error message innerHTML interpolation reflects pageName raw
description.description: `el.innerHTML = `<em class=

>Page not found: ${pageName}`` — `pageName` comes from `dataset.page` which is the macro `[[include:PageName]]` argument. If a malicious editor writes `[[include:]]`, the pageName contains HTML that is set as innerHTML. While DOMPurify sanitizes the parent render, this `innerHTML` write happens after and is NOT sanitized. Stored XSS via macro.
description.suggested_fix: Use `textContent` + DOM construction: `el.textContent = `Page not found: ${pageName}``. Better: build a real `<em>` element and set its `textContent`. Apply the same fix to line 62 (`Failed to load: ${pageName}`) and line 70.

description.title: Version list excludes content field but UI expects it
description.description: `select` block omits `content`. The `WikiVersionHistory.tsx` component renders `version.authorId.slice(0, 8)` (line 100) instead of `version.author.name`, indicating the type expects the author as an object — but the list API returns `author: { id, name, email, avatarUrl }` while the component reads `version.authorId`. Type mismatch: in the list response, `authorId` is not a field, but `author.id` is — the component would render `undefined.slice` and crash.
description.suggested_fix: Either expose `content: false` plus `authorId` as a string in the API, or update `WikiVersionHistory.tsx` to read `version.author.name` (with a fallback). Align the type contract in `types/wiki.ts`.

description.title: Comment content not sanitized on write, not rendered as Markdown
description.description: `ActivityComment.content` is stored as raw user input. GET returns it as-is, but no Markdown rendering on the client. If the comment UI uses `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` for comments (likely — they share the wiki components), this is an XSS vector distinct from the wiki content sanitizer.
description.suggested_fix: Render comments through `renderMarkdown` in the consuming component. Or mark them as plain text only and never allow HTML.

description.title: Search leaks data across projects when projectId is omitted
description.description: When `projectId` is not in the query, `projectFilter = {}` and the wiki/document/forum queries return rows from ALL projects the database knows about, then the response is sent to any authenticated user. The handler only checks `session?.user` — there is NO membership filter. A non-member of any project still gets the full search index back.
description.suggested_fix: Build the user's project-membership list (one Prisma call: `prisma.member.findMany({ where: { userId }, select: { projectId: true } })`) and intersect with the search filter: `projectFilter = { projectId: { in: userProjectIds } }`. System admins can pass empty to search all.

description.title: window.find() handler hijacks Ctrl+F browser shortcut
description.description: Lines 21-32: `document.addEventListener('keydown', ...)` calls `event.preventDefault()` on Ctrl+F / Cmd+F and invokes `window.find()`. `window.find()` is non-standard (Firefox ignores it, Safari deprecated it, Chrome behavior varies), and overriding the native Find dialog degrades UX for users who actually want the browser Find bar.
description.suggested_fix: Remove the handler entirely. Let the browser handle Ctrl+F natively. If in-page search is desired, build a custom UI panel.

description.title: TOC id generation can produce duplicate IDs causing broken anchor links
description.description: `extractTableOfContents` produces `id` by lowercasing and replacing non-alphanumerics with `-`. Two headings with the same text yield identical ids (HTML spec: duplicate ids are invalid). Additionally, ids derived from heading text can collide with the same slug used in a different page section. Anchors will scroll to the first match.
description.suggested_fix: Append a per-page counter suffix: `id_${baseId}_${counter}`. Or use `rehype-slug` plugin which is purpose-built and handles uniqueness.

description.title: Component renders version.authorId.slice but type expects author object
description.description: `version.authorId.slice(0, 8)` — but the WikiPageVersion type (`types/wiki.ts`) and the version list API return `author` (object), not `authorId`. `version.authorId` is `undefined` → `.slice` throws TypeError at runtime. This component is broken in production as soon as version history is rendered.
description.suggested_fix: Read `version.author?.name ?? version.authorId?.slice(0, 8) ?? 'Unknown'`. Or update the type so version records consistently expose `authorId: string` in the list response.

description.title: Upload handler accepts file but no client-side size/type validation
description.description: `onUpload({ file, ... })` is invoked with the raw File. No check on `file.size` (could be 10 GB → server timeout), no `file.type` allowlist (could upload `.exe` renamed to `.pdf`). The API endpoint at `/api/projects/[projectId]/documents/[id]/versions` is a metadata-only POST (no multipart parsing), so the actual file upload endpoint is missing entirely.
description.suggested_fix: Add `if (file.size > MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES)` client-side check, validate MIME type against an allowlist, then POST as multipart/form-data. Backend needs a real multipart parser (e.g. `formidable` or Next.js built-in `unstable_parseMultipartFormData`).

description.title: Recursive folder rendering has no depth limit — deep nesting crashes browser
description.description: `renderFolder` recurses with `depth + 1` for each child. If a folder cycle is created (see WIKI-17), the recursion is infinite and crashes the page. Even without cycles, deeply nested folders (>500 levels) blow the call stack.
description.suggested_fix: Add a `MAX_DEPTH = 50` cap: `if (depth >= MAX_DEPTH) return <DepthLimit />`. Combine with cycle prevention on the backend.

description.title: PATCH schema omits parentId — folder move not supported via project-scoped route
description.description: `UpdateFolderSchema = z.object({ name: ... })` does NOT allow `parentId`. Yet folder MOVE is a documented requirement (Phase 4 spec §3). The bulk `pages/api/documents/folders/[id]/index.ts` route DOES allow parentId in its schema. Inconsistent feature parity between routes.
description.suggested_fix: Add `parentId: z.string().cuid().nullable().optional()` to `UpdateFolderSchema`, then implement move logic with cycle + cross-project checks (see WIKI-17).

description.title: Version list response wraps data in wikiPageTitle but client expects flat array
description.description: Returns `{ wikiPageId, wikiPageTitle, versions: [...] }`. Component `WikiVersionHistory` expects `versions: WikiPageVersion[]` (flat array). Mismatch — component will show empty history unless parent unwraps.
description.suggested_fix: Standardize: return `{ success: true, data: { wikiPageId, versions } }` and update consumer OR return flat `versions` array. Pick one shape, document it in `types/wiki.ts`.

description.title: Document version create is metadata-only — no actual file upload
description.description: POST `/api/projects/[projectId]/documents/[id]/versions` accepts `{ fileName, fileSize, fileType, fileUrl }` and creates a version row. There is NO multipart body parsing — clients must pre-upload the file to a separate `/api/files` endpoint and pass the resulting URL. This is incomplete per Phase 4 spec §3.3 which requires real document upload with file storage. No size limit, no MIME check, no virus scan.
description.suggested_fix: Either implement multipart upload directly in this route (use Next.js bodyParser config + `formidable` or `busboy`), or document clearly that `/api/files` must be called first and pass `fileUrl` here. Add server-side size cap (e.g. 100 MB) and MIME allowlist.

description.title: No tests cover: slug collision, markdown XSS, version history, cycle detection
description.description: Test file (401 lines) only covers: 401 auth gate, 403 non-member, 404 not-found, 200 happy-path membership. Zero coverage for: (a) two pages with same slug in same project, (b) Markdown rendering with `
description.suggested_fix: Add tests: `describe('slug collision')` creating two pages with identical titles; `describe('markdown XSS')` posting `<script>alert(1)</script>` and asserting sanitized response; `describe('cycle prevention')` PATCHing page A to parent B then B to parent A; `describe('version restore')` POSTing `/restore` with valid version number.

description.title: No tests cover: folder nesting, multipart upload, cross-project folder move
description.description: Test file (409 lines) covers 401/403/404/200 happy paths and the assertXxx helpers. Missing: (a) folder move (parentId change) — bulk route allows it, project-scoped does not (WIKI-31), (b) cross-project folder/doc association (WIKI-15, WIKI-16), (c) folder delete with non-empty children (does it cascade or 400?), (d) file upload (multipart).
description.suggested_fix: Add tests: `describe('folder cross-project')` POSTing folder with foreign parentId and asserting 400; `describe('folder delete with children')` asserting 400; `describe('multipart upload')` if endpoint exists.

description.title: Missing wiki search endpoint (full-text) — partial implementation only
description.description: Search is implemented in `/api/search/index.ts` with a basic `contains` query. No full-text search, no ranking, no snippet highlighting, no pagination cursor. For wiki with hundreds of pages per project, this is unusable at scale (LIKE '%query%' on `content @db.Text` does a full table scan).
description.suggested_fix: Add a Postgres `tsvector` index on `(title, content)` and use `ts_rank` + `ts_headline` for results. Add a dedicated `GET /api/projects/[projectId]/wiki/search?q=...` endpoint with proper pagination and snippet highlighting.

description.title: Wiki diff between two versions not implemented
description.description: No endpoint computes the textual diff between two `WikiPageVersion.content` snapshots. The version history UI lets users view a single version but cannot show a side-by-side or unified diff (Phase 4 spec §2.7 implies this).
description.suggested_fix: Add `GET /api/projects/[projectId]/wiki/[slug]/diff?from=1&to=3` that returns unified diff using `diff` package or `jsdiff`. Or compute client-side (cheaper server load but bigger client bundle).

description.title: ALLOWED_URI_REGEXP allows arbitrary non-letter URI schemes
description.description: `/^(?:(?:https?|mailto|tel):|[^a-z]|[a-z+.-]+(?:[^a-z+.-:]|$))/i` permits ANY scheme starting with `a-z+.-` (so `data:`, `vbscript:` should be blocked — actually the negative lookahead `(?:[^a-z+.-:]|$)` requires the character AFTER the scheme to be NOT in the allowed scheme-char set, which DOES block `data:`-followed-by-anything). But it still allows obscure schemes like `custom-app:` if followed by a space. Verify with a test.
description.suggested_fix: Pin the regex to an explicit allowlist: `/^(?:https?|mailto|tel):/i` — reject everything else. Less risky than the regex.

description.title: renderMarkdown imported but is async — sync render would block paint
description.description: Client imports `renderMarkdown` from `@/lib/markdown`. The function is `async` (uses `unified.process` which is async). The component awaits it in useEffect, so paint is delayed on every render. No Suspense, no placeholder skeleton.
description.suggested_fix: Either accept the delay (current behavior is fine for short content) or move rendering to a Web Worker. For typical wiki pages <10 KB, the cost is negligible — document the limit in the component.

description.title: No pagination on version list — unbounded payload for heavily-edited pages
description.description: `findMany({ where: { wikiPageId: id } })` with no `take`/`skip` returns all versions. A wiki page edited 10,000 times returns 10,000 rows.
description.suggested_fix: Add `take: 50` + `skip` query params; add a `totalCount` in the response. Allow `?includeContent=false` to omit content blobs.

description.title: Bulk documents list does not filter soft-deleted rows
description.description: Bulk `pages/api/documents/index.ts` GET does not filter `deletedAt: null` — but the Document model (prisma/schema.prisma:671) has `deletedAt DateTime?` and `deletedBy String?`. The project-scoped route DOES filter (line 43). Inconsistent: bulk list returns soft-deleted rows, project-scoped list does not.
description.suggested_fix: Add `deletedAt: null` to the `where` clause in the bulk GET to match the project-scoped behavior. Same for bulk POST create — soft-delete audit already exists at the column level.

description.title: Bulk folder PATCH does not support parentId (folder move) — parity gap
description.description: Project-scoped `pages/api/projects/[projectId]/document-folders/[id]/index.ts` UpdateFolderSchema has ONLY `name` — no `parentId`. Bulk `pages/api/documents/folders/[id]/index.ts` accepts parentId. Feature parity gap. Spec implies folder move should work both ways.
description.suggested_fix: Add `parentId: z.string().cuid().nullable().optional()` to project-scoped UpdateFolderSchema, then implement move logic with cycle + cross-project checks (see WIKI-17).

description.title: Restore lacks permission granular check (wiki.restore) in scope check
description.description: Restore uses `requireProjectPermission(projectId, 'wiki.restore', session)` — but Phase 7 B-3 helpers add `assertWikiPageProjectMembership` for the GET/PATCH/DELETE. The restore route does not use the Phase 7 B-3 helper consistently — uses `requireProjectPermission` instead of `assertWikiPageBySlugProjectMembership`. This means an unauthenticated or non-member bypass is possible if `requireProjectPermission` is misconfigured. Verify by inspecting `lib/permissions/check.ts`.
description.suggested_fix: Refactor restore route to use `assertWikiPageBySlugProjectMembership` first (matching pattern of `pages/api/wiki/[id]/restore.ts`) then call `requireProjectPermission('wiki.restore')`.

description.title: Esc cancels without confirmation if content is dirty
description.description: `if (e.key === 'Escape') onCancel()` discards unsaved edits without warning. Users lose work on accidental Esc.
description.suggested_fix: Track dirty state (`content !== initialContent || title !== initialTitle`) and confirm via `window.confirm` before calling `onCancel`.

description.title: Slug validation regex not in zod schema — relies on generateSlug output only
description.description: `updateWikiPageSchema` does not validate `slug` field directly (slug is derived from title). But the slug field IS persisted in the DB on update. If `body.slug` is added later by a careless client, it bypasses validation.
description.suggested_fix: Use `z.object({ title: ..., content: ..., slug: z.string().regex(/^[a-z0-9-]+$/).max(80).optional(), parentId: ... })` and explicitly reject other slug values. Better: derive slug from title only and never accept slug from body.
